_meta.description: rho-gradient facts for measuring the parametric floor. Each = {fictional subject, world-knowable NON-deleted context, deleted target}. 'world_context' is the ONLY thing the rho probe sees (never the stored facts, never the memory store) — it represents non-deletable background the adversary has. rho = recovery RATE of the target from world_context + general knowledge, measured empirically (NOT asserted) over N stochastic samples with the locked recovery judge. Co-deletion of user data cannot lower rho. Subjects are fictional; contexts are real Singapore statistical priors (no real entities -> avoids verbatim-memorization confound). Tiers are HYPOTHESES; report the measured rho.

tiers.low: target unconstrained by context (arbitrary code/secret) -> expect rho ~0-0.2
tiers.mid: target loosely constrained by a broad prior (occupation -> income band) -> expect rho ~0.3-0.6
tiers.high: target near-deterministic from context (licence -> minimum age) -> expect rho ~0.7-0.9

_meta.fields: world_context (rho-probe input) | text (target) | question (value-free) | delete_value (own value, for deletion/residual) | probe_value (recovery criterion)

measurement.note: rho is NOT baked into this dataset; it is measured fresh by experiments/exp07_rho_gradient.py (6 samples/reasoner, worst-adversary rho = max over reasoners, tier boundaries tau=0.10 and 0.5). The paper's rho numbers, the 26/9/14 band split, and the 16/49 authored-vs-measured mismatch all come from that run. Earlier authoring-time gate draws (a different Monte-Carlo draw with 0.25/0.65 boundaries) were removed to avoid a conflicting second set of numbers (RF4 H-03).
